commit.message:
EXP-1201-1210: Conformal PIs, full stack R²=0.664 CV, horizon ensemble R²=0.839

Breakthroughs:
- EXP-1201: Conformal PIs achieve 80% coverage, 75.5 mg/dL width (AR)
- EXP-1202: Full stack (combined+AR+online) 5-fold CV = R²=0.664 NEW SOTA
- EXP-1203: AR benefit scales with horizon — 120min +0.430 lift!
- EXP-1210: Horizon ensemble+AR = R²=0.839 (needs CV validation)

Diagnostics:
- EXP-1205: Patient h has 64% NaN (root cause), k has 95% TIR (low signal)
- EXP-1206: 2/11 patients data-starved (e, j), most saturate at 40-60%
- EXP-1209: ACF(1)=0.474 explains AR improvement, positive skew=0.45

Dead ends: Kalman filter (0.221), temporal features (-0.003),
regime models (-0.014), feature interactions (-0.002)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: responsive multi-viewport capture suite

Capture screenshots at multiple named breakpoints in a single call with
optional pairwise pixel diffs between consecutive viewports.

**Types (packages/shared/src/browser.ts)**
- VIEWPORT_PRESETS const: 8 named presets from mobile-sm (375x667) to
  wide (1920x1080), each with width/height/mobile/label
- ViewportPresetName: keyof VIEWPORT_PRESETS
- ViewportPreset: { name, width, height, mobile, label }
- ViewportCapture: { preset, snapshotId, screenshot }
- ViewportSuiteReport: { tabId, runId, captures[], diffs[], capturedAt }

**TabManager (apps/desktop/src/main/tab-manager.ts)**
- captureViewportSuite(tabId, presets?, includeDiffs?)
  - Saves existing emulated viewport before run; restores after

    captureNamedScreenshot (snapshotId: tabId__preset__runId)
  - includeDiffs: pairwise diffScreenshots on consecutive captures
  - Restores original viewport or clears override if none was set

**AgentServer (apps/desktop/src/main/agent-server.ts)**
- POST /api/tabs/:id/viewport-suite { presets?, includeDiffs? }

**Agent SDK (packages/agent-sdk/src/index.ts)**
 ViewportSuiteReport
- Exports: ViewportPresetName, ViewportSuiteReport, VIEWPORT_PRESETS

**Docs**
- docs/dev-team-features.md: updated section 9 with full API docs,
  preset reference table, and usage example

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add syslog notifier for Parliament alerts (fixes #2263) (#3865)

Add a new syslog notifier type that sends alerts to a
user-provided syslog server. Supports UDP, TCP, and TLS transport
protocols with configurable facility, severity, and tag fields.
Messages are formatted per RFC 5424.

Uses Node.js built-in dgram/net/tls modules - no new dependencies.
The notifier plugin system auto-discovers the new file.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Docs: release robopsychology v1.6

Add two insights from CIRIS (github.com/CIRISAI/CIRISAgent):

1. Diagnostic ratchet (Rule 4): longer diagnostic sequences make
   genuine transparency easier and performed transparency harder —
   each honest answer is cheap (references prior behavior), each
   fabricated answer is expensive (must stay consistent with growing
   history). Strengthens the case for running the full sequence.

2. Diagnostic diversity check (prompt 4.3): inspired by CIRIS IDMA
   echo-chamber detection — are the model's multiple explanations
   genuinely independent perspectives or reworded echoes of the
   same underlying pattern?

Acknowledge CIRIS and Eric Moore in the Background section.
Prompt count: 13 → 14. Full sequence updated.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: robopsychology

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

example: set device cookie httpOnly (#868)

Fix for
[https://github.com/zitadel/oidc/security/code-scanning/5](https://github.com/zitadel/oidc/security/code-scanning/5)

To fix the problem in general, any cookie that stores authentication or
authorization-related data and is not intended to be accessed by
client-side JavaScript should be created with `HttpOnly: true`. In Go,
this is done by setting the `HttpOnly` field on the `http.Cookie`
struct.

In this specific file, the cookie is created in `loginHandler` at lines
164–169. We should update the `http.Cookie` literal to include
`HttpOnly: true`. This leaves the rest of the behavior unchanged: same
name, value, path, and use of `securecookie` for encoding. No other code
changes are required, and no new imports are needed, because `HttpOnly`
is already part of the `net/http` standard library’s `Cookie` struct.

Concretely:
- In `example/server/exampleop/device.go`, locate the cookie creation in
`loginHandler`.
- Modify the struct literal for `cookie` to add `HttpOnly: true`.
- Keep `Name`, `Value`, and `Path` as they are.

This single change will address all three CodeQL alert variants, since
they all refer to the same cookie.


_Suggested fixes powered by Copilot Autofix. Review carefully before
merging._

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <62310815+github-advanced-security[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Gayathri Vijayan <66356931+grvijayan@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial EFN MCP server implementation

MCP server for EFN (Ekonomikanalen) providing Swedish financial news,
expert analysis, and market commentary via 10 tools:

News: get_latest_news, get_article, get_breaking_news,
      search_news_by_topic, list_categories
Analysis: get_analyst_picks, get_market_commentary, get_report_commentary
Media: get_play_episodes, get_podcast_episodes

Built with FastMCP 3.0, Python 3.12, httpx, BeautifulSoup.
Includes 3 prompts, 2 resources, and 29 unit tests.

Designed to complement avanza-mcp (quantitative data) with
qualitative editorial content — zero overlap.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: comment quality analysis report (CC vs CN)

* docs: add comment quality audit report (CC vs CN analysis)

Scanned 172 .ts files (66,735 LOC) in apps/desktop/main/src/ against
ARCHITECTURE.md §四 5-dimension comment quality framework.

Overall score: 38/100
- D1 Safety Defaults: 12/20 (68 comments, but 98% catch blocks uncovered)
- D2 Performance: 10/20 (50 comments, missing timing annotations)
- D3 Thresholds: 2/20 (9/475 magic numbers explained)
- D4 Intent Preservation: 10/20 (orchestrator exemplary, not replicated)
- D5 Boundary/@module: 4/20 (0/20 services have @module)

Key finding: CN's "Why:" JSDoc pattern (204 instances) is a genuine
strength that rivals CC patterns, but systematic coverage of thresholds,
catch blocks, and module boundaries remains critically low.

feat: console/network logs, network mock intercept, visual snapshot diff

Three new dev-team features exposed via AgentServer REST + BrowserClient SDK:

**Console + Network Log Capture**
- New types: ConsoleLogEntry, NetworkRequestEntry, TabLogSnapshot (perception.ts)
- Enable CDP Runtime/Log/Network domains on first snapshot per tab
- Buffer events in TabRecord; clear on main-frame navigation

 networkRequests
 TabLogSnapshot
 clears buffers
- SDK: getLogs(tabId), clearLogs(tabId)

**Network Mock / Intercept**
- New type: NetworkInterceptRule in substrate.ts (urlPattern, method, responseStatus,
  responseHeaders, responseBody)
- enableNetworkMock: Fetch.enable with wildcard pattern; each Fetch.requestPaused
 Fetch.fulfillRequest (mock) or Fetch.continueRequest (pass)
- matchesMockRule: supports * wildcard globs and /regex/flags syntax
 enables
 current rules
 disables
- SDK: enableNetworkMock, disableNetworkMock, getNetworkMockRules

**Visual Snapshot Diff**
- New type: ScreenshotDiff in browser.ts (diffPixelCount, diffPercentage,
  diffImageData base64 PNG)
- screenshotCache Map<string, PageScreenshot> on TabManager
- captureNamedScreenshot: captures + stores under caller-supplied snapshotId
- diffScreenshots: Electron nativeImage BGRA pixel comparison ()))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))),tolerance
  changed pixels highlighted red in diff overlay image
 PageScreenshot
 ScreenshotDiff
- SDK: captureNamedScreenshot, diffScreenshots

**Docs**

  implementation notes, and backlog of 8 more features

[BRIC] Add tab-based scenario editor with save dialog

Introduce a tab bar to the edit page so users can work on multiple
scenarios side by side. Each tab independently tracks its own
Blockly workspace state, startPlay IDs, guard settings, and errors.
Switching or closing a tab serializes and restores the workspace
transparently.

Replace the inline scenario-name input with a modal save dialog that
opens when "Save Scenario" is clicked. Pressing Enter confirms and
Escape or outside-click cancels without saving.

Add prompt_tab specification document describing the tab UI layout,
open-by-double-click behavior, and per-tab state contract.

Update style.css with file-tab-shaped styles distinct from action
buttons, including active highlight, overflow scrolling, and a
close button with hover effect.

commit.message:
Pass WilsonVersion for IdentityModel external package build (#1629)

The IdentityModel repo's build/common.props computes FileVersion and
AssemblyVersion from WilsonVersion, not Version. Without passing
WilsonVersion, the build falls back to the hardcoded WilsonCurrentVersion
(8.0.0) instead of the release version (8.0.1), resulting in incorrect
file versions in the built DLLs.

Fix dotnet/source-build#5508

commit.message:
v2.1.0: Fix slot collision, dispose race, CQ overflow recovery

Slot collision (High):
- Add generation counter to UserData (16-bit, per-slot). CQEs from
  previous connections are detected and silently discarded.

DisposeAsync race (High):
- Add volatile _disposing flag. CompleteSend checks it before accessing
  shared state, preventing double-dispose with Kestrel's thread.

CQ overflow recovery (Critical):
- On overflow detection, scan all connections with stale HasSendInFlight/
  HasRecvInFlight. Force-dispose handles, close sockets, signal send
  loops to break. Recovers gracefully instead of leaking resources.

Also:
- UnsafeInlineScheduling option (default true, set false for safety)
- Partial send retry loop (handles short kernel writes)
- MemoryHandle leak fix (dispose on any failure between Pin and SQE)
- Infinite error loop fix (force-advance CQ after 5 consecutive errors)
- Remove dead code (PendingSend, OnSendComplete, PooledSendCompletion)

Lab benchmark: 2,559,694 RPS @ 1024c TC=28 (no regression from v2.0)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Stabilize release workflow checks

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(email): sync daily quotas from provider APIs before sending

Replace in-memory-only counters with real usage data from each
provider's API at the start of every cascade run:
- Mailgun: GET /v3/{domain}/stats/total?event=accepted&duration=1d
- Mailjet: GET /v3/REST/statcounters (daily resolution)
- Unosend: GET /v1/emails (filter by today's date)
- Resend: GET /emails (filter by today's date)

This ensures accurate quota tracking across separate workflow runs
instead of relying on 429 responses to detect exhausted limits.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: Round 3 codebase cleanup — security fixes, endpoint dedup, frontend types

Phase 1: Security — redact redis_url from /cache/config, remove internal cache job endpoints, fix missing await in batch cancel
Phase 2: Remove duplicate batch endpoints from processing_routes (~45 lines)
Phase 3: Consolidate graph search — remove /search/entities, /search/frames, /search/advanced; enhance /search/hybrid with video_ids, entity_types, graph expansion toggle
Phase 4: Standardize all 17+ dual video_id OR patterns to single v.video_id across 6 files
Phase 5: Frontend type dedup — replace 7 duplicate Scene/Chapter/TranscriptSegment definitions with imports from @/types; remove debug console.logs; add null-safety for scene.duration
Phase 6: Frontend component audit — confirmed 'use client' removal has zero bundle benefit; deferred
Phase 7: Remove 5 dead endpoints (ffmpeg, ffmpeg/batch, 2x /search, jobs/submit); clean orphaned imports and dead test functions

30 files changed, +152/-1089 lines, 1184 tests passing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: QPrisma
repository.description: QPrisma is an enterprise-grade, AI-powered multimedia processing platform designed to unlock insights from video and image content. By combining computer vision, Large Language Models (LLMs), and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), QPrisma transforms unstructured media into searchable, actionable knowledge.

commit.message:
fix(demo): set actorId on payments and expenses in demo seed

Every demo login wipes and re-seeds via demo-seed.ts. Without actorId,
the Cash Accountability section would always show empty. Now all seeded
payments and expenses are attributed to the demo owner (ownerId).

Initial commit: vox-linux v0.1.0

Voice input for Linux — type anywhere or talk to Copilot CLI.

Features:
- Two hotkey modes: voice-type (Super+V) and voice-copilot (Super+C)
- Fully local transcription via whisper.cpp (no cloud, no API key)
- Auto-detects X11/Wayland, PipeWire/PulseAudio/ALSA
- Terminal detection (Ctrl+Shift+V vs Ctrl+V) for correct paste
- One-command installer: ./install.sh
- GNOME and KDE hotkey registration
- Clipboard save/restore after injection
- ydotool/wtype/xdotool backends with graceful fallback

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: vox-linux
repository.description: Talk to your Linux instead of typing
